name: Coffin v. United States
summary: Established presumption of innocence as fundamental principle of American criminal law; burden of proof rests on prosecution to prove guilt beyond reasonable doubt.
holding: Presumption of innocence is not mere rule of evidence but fundamental principle of criminal justice; prosecution bears burden of proving guilt beyond reasonable doubt.
relevance_to_audit: Establishes foundation for burden of proof in administrative enforcement actions; relevant to presumptions in regulatory proceedings and evidentiary standards.
procedural_posture: Criminal conviction appealed to Supreme Court
